title: Scorecard Row Height and Status Cell Color Coding
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing the scorecard, I want status cells to be color-coded and rows to have a consistent minimum height, so that I can quickly scan migration status at a glance.
description:
Apply design refinements to the scorecard on select.php to improve visual readability.

## Requirements
- Set minimum row height to 60px on select.php
- Apply background colors to status cells:
  - Complete: `#defaf1`
  - Blocked: `#fff7e4`
  - In Progress: `#edf6ff`
- Hardcode status column widths in pixels for consistency
- Apply a distinct CSS class for hyperlinks inside the scorecard grid
acceptance_criteria: Scorecard rows have a minimum height of 60px
Complete status cells display with #defaf1 background
Blocked status cells display with #fff7e4 background
In Progress status cells display with #edf6ff background
Status column widths are set to fixed pixel values
Long text content in cells causes row height to expand beyond 60px rather than clipping
ux_changes: Color-coded status cells on the scorecard (green for Complete, yellow for Blocked, blue for In Progress) with consistent minimum row height and fixed-width status columns.
implementation_notes: Min-height was implemented as a CSS minimum rather than a fixed height to allow rows with long text to expand naturally. Status column widths were hardcoded in pixels for visual consistency.
comment_summary: Developer implemented status colors and row height. Discussion around min-height vs fixed height concluded that min-height was preferable to avoid text clipping. Status column widths were set to reasonable pixel values.
